title: Crypto.com Secures MVP Preparatory License from Dubai's Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority
summary: On 20 March 2023, VARA -- the Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority, which licenses and supervises virtual asset activity in the Emirate of Dubai outside the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) free zone -- confirmed that Crypto.com had received an MVP Preparatory License. VARA describes the license's preparatory stage as letting an approved licensee complete the pre-conditions required before undertaking full MVP market operations under the VARA regime, rather than authorising it to operate immediately. VARA states its review covered Crypto.com's key personnel, governance procedures, AML/CFT capabilities, and KYC/UBO policies. Once licensed to operate, VARA says Crypto.com will be able to extend its regulated activities to crypto exchange services (spot and derivatives), brokerage, margin/leverage trading, and OTC settlement offerings for institutional investors. The license followed an earlier provisional approval VARA granted Crypto.com in June 2022.
why_it_matters: VARA is the authority that licenses and supervises crypto exchanges, brokers, and other virtual-asset businesses operating in Dubai outside the DIFC free zone, and an MVP Preparatory License like this one is a required staging step under its licensing framework before an approved firm can actually begin operating those services in the market.

citations.quote: confirmed today that it has received its MVP Preparatory License
